documentation:
# pyCOREPOS

A Python interface to the [CORE POS](https://github.com/CORE-POS)
system.

functions.name: corepos.db.util.get_last_card_number
documentation:
Convenience function, to return the "last" (max) value for the
`custdata.CardNo` field, for use when generating new values.

functions.name: corepos.db.util.table_exists
documentation:
Determine if a table exists in the database.

```
param session

SQLAlchemy session object, opened against the database in
question.

param model_class

The model class associated with the table in question.

returns

Boolean indicating if the table exists.
```

classes.name: corepos.api.CoreAPIError
documentation: Base class for errors coming from the CORE API proper.

classes.name: corepos.api.CoreWebAPI

methods.name: corepos.api.CoreWebAPI.post
documentation:
Issue a POST request to the API, with the given `params`. If not
specified, `method` will be CORE's `FannieEntity` webservice.

methods.name: corepos.api.CoreWebAPI.parse_response
documentation:
Generic method to "parse" a response from the API.  Really this just
converts the JSON to a dict (etc.), and then checks for error.  If an
error is found in the response, it will be raised here.

methods.name: corepos.api.CoreWebAPI.get_member_types
documentation:
Fetch all Member Type records from CORE.

:returns: A (potentially empty) list of member type dict records.

methods.name: corepos.api.CoreWebAPI.get_members
documentation:
Fetch all Member records from CORE.

:returns: A (potentially empty) list of member dict records.

methods.name: corepos.api.CoreWebAPI.get_member
documentation:
Fetch an existing Member record from CORE.

```
returns

Either a member dict record, or None.
```

methods.name: corepos.api.CoreWebAPI.set_member
documentation:
Update an existing Member record in CORE.

```
returns

Boolean indicating success of the operation.
```

:::{warning}
Only simple updates have been attempted thus far; have yet to try
creation or deletion. Neither of those should be expected to work.
:::

methods.name: corepos.api.CoreWebAPI.get_employees
documentation:
Fetch some or all of Employee records from CORE.

:returns: A (potentially empty) list of employee dict records.

methods.name: corepos.api.CoreWebAPI.get_employee
documentation:
Fetch an existing Employee record from CORE.

```
returns

Either a employee dict record, or None.
```

methods.name: corepos.api.CoreWebAPI.get_stores
documentation:
Fetch some or all of Store records from CORE.

```
returns

A (potentially empty) list of store dict records.
```

To fetch all stores:

```
api.get_stores()
```

To fetch only stores named "Headquarters":

```
api.get_stores(description='Headquarters')
```

methods.name: corepos.api.CoreWebAPI.get_departments
documentation:
Fetch some or all of Department records from CORE.

```
returns

A (potentially empty) list of department dict records.
```

To fetch all departments:

```
api.get_departments()
```

To fetch only departments named "Grocery":

```
api.get_departments(dept_name='Grocery')
```

methods.name: corepos.api.CoreWebAPI.get_department
documentation:
Fetch an existing Department record from CORE.

```
returns

Either a department dict record, or None.
```

methods.name: corepos.api.CoreWebAPI.set_department
documentation:
Update an existing Department record in CORE.

```
returns

Boolean indicating success of the operation.
```

:::{note}
Currently this is being used to create a *new* department also. CORE's
`departments` table does not use auto-increment for its PK, which
means we must provide one even when creating; therefore this method
may be used for that.
:::

methods.name: corepos.api.CoreWebAPI.get_subdepartments
documentation:
Fetch some or all of Subdepartment records from CORE.

```
returns

A (potentially empty) list of subdepartment dict records.
```

To fetch all subdepartments:

```
api.get_subdepartments()
```

To fetch only subdepartments named "Grocery":

```
api.get_subdepartments(subdept_name='Grocery')
```

methods.name: corepos.api.CoreWebAPI.get_subdepartment
documentation:
Fetch an existing Subdepartment record from CORE.

```
returns

Either a subdepartment dict record, or None.
```

methods.name: corepos.api.CoreWebAPI.set_subdepartment
documentation:
Update an existing Subdepartment record in CORE.

```
returns

Boolean indicating success of the operation.
```

:::{note}
Currently this is being used to create a *new* subdepartment also. CORE's
`subdepartments` table does not use auto-increment for its PK, which
means we must provide one even when creating; therefore this method
may be used for that.
:::

methods.name: corepos.api.CoreWebAPI.get_vendors
documentation:
Fetch some or all of Vendor records from CORE.

```
returns

A (potentially empty) list of vendor dict records.
```

To fetch all vendors:

```
api.get_vendors()
```

To fetch only vendors named "UNFI":

```
api.get_vendors(vendorName='UNFI')
```

methods.name: corepos.api.CoreWebAPI.get_vendor
documentation:
Fetch an existing Vendor record from CORE.

```
returns

Either a vendor dict record, or None.
```

methods.name: corepos.api.CoreWebAPI.set_vendor
documentation:
Update an existing Vendor record in CORE.

```
returns

Boolean indicating success of the operation.
```

:::{note}
Currently this is being used to create a *new* vendor also. CORE's
`vendors` table does not use auto-increment for its PK, which
means we must provide one even when creating; therefore this method
may be used for that.
:::

methods.name: corepos.api.CoreWebAPI.get_products
documentation:
Fetch some or all of Product records from CORE.

```
returns

A (potentially empty) list of product dict records.
```

To fetch all products:

```
api.get_products()
```

To fetch only products with brand name "Braggs":

```
api.get_products(brand='Braggs')
```

methods.name: corepos.api.CoreWebAPI.get_product
documentation:
Fetch an existing Product record from CORE.

```
returns

Either a product dict record, or None.
```

methods.name: corepos.api.CoreWebAPI.set_product
documentation:
Update an existing Product record in CORE.

```
returns

Boolean indicating success of the operation.
```

:::{note}
Currently this is being used to create a *new* product also. CORE's
`products` table does not use auto-increment for its PK, which
means we must provide one even when creating; therefore this method
may be used for that.
:::

methods.name: corepos.api.CoreWebAPI.get_vendor_items
documentation:
Fetch some or all of VendorItem records from CORE.

```
returns

A (potentially empty) list of vendor item dict records.
```

To fetch all vendor items:

```
api.get_vendor_items()
```

To fetch only products with brand name "Braggs":

```
api.get_vendor_items(brand='Braggs')
```

methods.name: corepos.api.CoreWebAPI.get_vendor_item
documentation:
Fetch an existing VendorItem record from CORE.

```
returns

Either a vendor item dict record, or None.
```

methods.name: corepos.api.CoreWebAPI.set_vendor_item
documentation:
Update an existing VendorItem record in CORE.

```
returns

Boolean indicating success of the operation.
```

:::{note}
Currently this is being used to create a *new* product also. CORE's
`vendorItems` table does not use auto-increment for its PK, which
means we must provide one even when creating; therefore this method
may be used for that.
:::

Client implementation for the CORE webservices API.

```
param str url

URL to the CORE webservices API,
e.g. 'http://localhost/fannie/ws/'

param bool verify

How to handle certificate validation for HTTPS
URLs.  This value is passed as-is to the requests library,
so see those docs for more info.  The default value for this is
True because the assumption is that security should be on
by default.  Set it to False in order to disable validation
entirely, e.g. for self-signed certs.  (This may also be needed
for basic HTTP URLs?)  Other values may be possible also; again
see the requests docs for more info.

param htdigest_username

Username for htdigest authentication, if
applicable.

param htdigest_password

Password for htdigest authentication, if
applicable.
```

classes.name: corepos.db.office_op.model.StringableDateTime
documentation:
Sort of a hack, to let us string-ify certain DateTime values when
generating "raw" SQL output.

cf. https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/14/faq/sqlexpressions.html#rendering-bound-parameters-inline

methods.name: corepos.db.office_op.model.TaxRate.__str__
documentation: TODO: this was not in some older DBs
sales_code = sa.Column('salesCode', sa.Integer(), nullable=True)

Represents a tax rate.  Note that this may be a "combo" of various local
tax rates / levels.

classes.name: corepos.db.office_op.model.TaxRateComponent
documentation: Represents a "component" of a tax rate.

classes.name: corepos.db.office_op.model.LikeCode
documentation: Represents a "like code" for sake of product pricing.

classes.name: corepos.db.office_op.model.OriginCountry
documentation: Represents a country which relates to the "origin" for some product(s).

classes.name: corepos.db.office_op.model.OriginStateProv
documentation: Represents a state/province which relates to the "origin" for some product(s).

classes.name: corepos.db.office_op.model.OriginCustomRegion
documentation: Represents a custom region which relates to the "origin" for some product(s).

classes.name: corepos.db.office_op.model.Origin
documentation: Represents a location which is the "origin" for some product(s).

classes.name: corepos.db.office_op.model.Product

methods.name: corepos.db.office_op.model.Product.complete_size
documentation:
Returns the "complete" size string for the product. This is based on
the {attr}`size` and {attr}`unit_of_measure` attributes.
Represents a product, purchased and/or sold by the organization.

classes.name: corepos.db.office_op.model.ProductLikeCode
documentation: Represents the association between a product and like code.

documentation: Represents a product flag attribute.

classes.name: corepos.db.office_op.model.MemberInfo

methods.name: corepos.db.office_op.model.MemberInfo.split_street
documentation:
Tries to split the {attr}`street` attribute into 2 separate lines, e.g.
"street1" and "street2" style. Always returns a 2-tuple even if the
second line would be empty.
